reforms.description: The revised 2000 Basic Act assigns responsibility for R&D in the telecommunications sector to the Ministry of Information and Communication (MIC), which oversees the Korea Electronics and Telecommunication Research Institute. This reform mandates telecommunications companies to allocate a percentage of their revenue towards R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By mandating R&D spending, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and technological development in the telecommunications sector, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The MIC is responsible for R&D in the telecommunications sector and oversees the funding mechanisms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of R&D in the telecommunications sector, establishing a framework for sustained investment in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Under the revised 2000 Basic Act, MIC is responsible for R&D in the telecommunication sector and runs the Korea Electronics and Telecommunication Research Institute.

reforms.description: The Foreign Investment Promotion Act (FIPA) expanded tax incentives for foreign direct investment (FDI) by increasing the number of eligible advanced technology sectors and extending the duration of tax exemptions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting high-technology FDI, the reform is expected to enhance domestic innovation capabilities and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The FIPA was enacted to enhance tax incentives for FDI.
reforms.importance_rationale: The FIPA represents a significant structural change in Korea's approach to attracting foreign investment, particularly in high-technology sectors, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Under the FIPA, the number has been expanded to 436.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase science and technology expenditures to develop a knowledge-based economy, raising the share of R&D outlays in the central government budget from 3.7% to 4.1%, with a target of reaching 5% by 2003.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and contribute to economic growth through the development of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The planned rise in science and technology expenditures was mentioned as part of the government's budget priorities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic development in Korea.
reforms.source_quote: The planned rise would boost the share of R&D outlays in the central government budget from 3.7 to 4.1 per cent, in line with the objective of reaching 5 per cent by 2003.

reforms.description: The Korean government introduced a legal framework for venture businesses in 1997, aimed at facilitating the start-up of new technology-based firms and enhancing their access to financing, technology, and manpower.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting venture businesses, the reform encourages innovation and entrepreneurship, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legal category for venture businesses was introduced and recognized 3600 firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support for small, innovative firms, which is crucial for fostering a dynamic economy.
reforms.source_quote: The 1997 law... was intended to facilitate the start-up of new venture businesses, enhance the availability of financing, technology and manpower, as well as physical facilities, and to provide tax incentives.

reforms.description: The government launched a package of special measures in October 1997 to promote venture businesses, which includes facilitating start-ups, providing financing sources, and encouraging manpower and technology development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures aim to enhance the entrepreneurial environment, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth through the establishment of new ventures.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched a package of special measures in October 1997 to promote venture business.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards supporting entrepreneurship and innovation in Korea, which is crucial for the development of a knowledge-based economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched a package of special measures in October 1997 to promote venture business.

reforms.description: The Basic Act on Administrative Regulation was introduced to reform government regulations, aiming to promote private-sector initiative and creativity while enhancing national competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By eliminating unnecessary regulations, the reform is expected to enhance competition and stimulate private sector growth, leading to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform achieved the target of abolishing half of the existing regulations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in regulatory policy, aimed at enhancing the overall competitiveness of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Basic Act established the Regulatory Reform Committee (RRC) in the Prime Minister’s office... with the task of eliminating half of the 11095 existing regulations.

reforms.description: Korea's ten-year plan to introduce market forces into the electricity sector aims to privatize and create competition within the industry, which has been dominated by a state-owned enterprise.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering competition and privatization, the reform is expected to enhance efficiency and innovation in the electricity sector, ultimately benefiting consumers.
reforms.status_evidence: Korea announced a ten-year plan in 1999 to introduce market forces into its electricity sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a fundamental shift in the structure of the electricity market, aiming to enhance competition and efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: Korea announced a ten-year plan in 1999 to introduce market forces into its electricity sector.

reforms.description: The rule mandating that telecommunication operators devote 1.8 to 3 percent of their revenues for R&D of domestic producers should be abolished, allowing firms to allocate resources more efficiently.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Removing the revenue mandate allows firms to invest in R&D according to their strategic priorities, potentially leading to more innovative outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The rule is proposed for abolition to enhance competition and efficiency.
reforms.importance_rationale: The abolition of the revenue mandate represents a significant shift in policy that could enhance the flexibility of firms in allocating R&D resources.
reforms.source_quote: The rule mandating that telecommunication operators devote 1.8 to 3 percent of their revenues for R&D of domestic producers should be abolished.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced ambitious targets to enhance the science and technology base to the level of G-7 nations, improve educational standards to OECD norms, and upgrade the nation’s information infrastructure significantly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and technology infrastructure is expected to enhance human capital and innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced ambitious targets in January 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a structural change aimed at transforming Korea into a knowledge-based economy, which is crucial for long-term economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced ambitious targets in January 2000 to accelerate the development of a knowledge-based economy in Korea.

reforms.description: The Korean government aims to increase R&D spending from 3.7% to 5% of its total budget by 2003, focusing on enhancing the role of government research institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and support the development of new technologies, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government’s goal is to increase R&D spending, which rises from 3.7 to 4.1% of its total budget in 2000, to 5% by 2003.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is essential for fostering innovation and technological advancement.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s goal is to increase R&D spending, which rises from 3.7 to 4.1% of its total budget in 2000, to 5% by 2003.

reforms.description: Korea has announced a three-year plan to upgrade its information infrastructure, aiming to connect all elementary and lower secondary schools to the Internet by 2000 and significantly increase the number of Internet users.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving information infrastructure enhances access to technology, reduces transaction costs, and supports the development of new products and services, thereby boosting economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The recently announced three-year plan has ambitious targets to upgrade the information infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant investment in the foundational infrastructure necessary for a knowledge-based economy, with long-term implications for education and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The recently announced three-year plan has ambitious targets to upgrade the information infrastructure to a level that would rank in the top ten globally.

reforms.description: The Korean government launched a 'Bond Market Stabilisation Fund' using 20 trillion won to support bond prices during the financial crisis triggered by the Daewoo insolvency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aimed to stabilize the bond market, which is crucial for maintaining investor confidence and supporting overall economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government created the Bond Market Stabilisation Fund in September 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform was a significant intervention in the financial market aimed at restoring stability and confidence, which is essential for economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The government, in light of instability in financial markets, launches the 'Bond Market Stabilisation Fund' using 20 trillion won supplied by banks and insurance companies.

reforms.description: The government announced a 1 trillion won fund for venture investment to stimulate innovation and support the growth of new businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding for venture investments, the initiative aims to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a 1 trillion won fund for venture investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant but targeted effort to enhance the startup ecosystem, which is crucial for long-term economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The government announces a 1 trillion won fund for venture investment.
